prompt: The Prototype clone shares mutable state with the original. What is the bug?
choices: Cloneable requires a copy constructor, not an override of clone()
super.clone() performs a shallow copy; both p1.data and p2.data reference the same array, so mutating p2.data[0] also changes p1.data[0]
clone() must be declared static to create a new instance
int arrays cannot be cloned in Java
explanation: Object.clone() does a shallow copy — it copies the data reference, not the array itself. Both prototypes share the same int[]. Mutating p2.data[0] changes p1.data[0] to 99. Fix: in clone(), perform a deep copy: Prototype copy = (Prototype) super.clone(); copy.data = data.clone(); return copy;
